message:
fix(engine): strengthen deterministic hashing across doctor preflight, proof ingestion, runtime gate, and timescale certificates

Comprehensive hashing correctness pass — every content hash must
include all semantically meaningful fields to prevent collisions
between structurally different objects:

- react_doctor_preflight.rs: Introduce length-prefixed hash helpers
  (hash_u64, hash_str, hash_string_vec, hash_string_set) to prevent
  concatenation collisions. DoctorCheck::content_hash() now includes
  remediation, related_mismatch_ids, and tags. GuidanceEntry::
  content_hash() now includes priority, category, severity, and
  originating_check_ids. run_preflight result hash includes
  entries_analyzed count. build_support_bundle hash now includes entry
  categories, all summary counters, aggregate_score, is_ready, and
  guidance hashes. GuidanceEntry.guidance_hash is now computed from
  the full payload via content_hash() instead of just id+title.
  Add tests: doctor_check_content_hash_changes_when_payload_changes,
  preflight_result_hash_changes_when_entries_analyzed_changes,
  guidance_content_hash_changes_when_metadata_changes,
  generate_guidance_sets_hash_from_full_payload,
  support_bundle_hash_changes_when_guidance_payload_changes.

- proof_ingestion.rs: ProofInput::canonical_bytes() now includes
  issuer_signature and payload. Enum fields use to_string() instead
  of `as u8` cast (which silently truncates discriminant values).
  OptimizerHypothesis::canonical_bytes() includes optimization_class
  debug repr and uses string-based enum representation for kind/risk.

- runtime_comparison_gate.rs: Evidence hash now includes blocker
  count and reproducibility entry count so two runs with different
  blockers but same benchmark totals no longer collide.

- timescale_separation_certificate.rs: Fix potential u64 overflow
  in timescale ratio computation by widening operands to u128 before
  the multiplication by 1_000_000 — without this, two large u64
  observation counts could silently wrap, producing incorrect ratios.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
